element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns to play four-note tunes (drafts) as core mechanic
Limited note range initially; expanded as game progresses
Player observes objects with desired qualities to learn corresponding drafts (e.g., observing sharpening teaches 'Sharpening' draft)
Some drafts reverse by playing notes backwards (e.g., 'Dye' reversed becomes 'Bleach'); palindromic drafts cannot be reversed

element_introduction_order.element: Additional musical notes (beyond C, D, E)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Expanded note availability unlocks more powerful spells
Puzzles require understanding draft effects and creative application

typical_tension_triggers: Narrative stakes (Elders' decree on Bobbin's fate)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Gradual mechanical expansion with story-driven tension; player agency increases as note vocabulary and spell repertoire grow

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Objects contain learnable draft knowledge through examination; observation mechanic replaces traditional item collection

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-like; three starting notes (C, D, E); basic draft learning and application
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progressive note unlocks expand puzzle possibilities; reversible draft mechanics introduce complexity
difficulty_curve.late_game: Full musical vocabulary enables complex spell combinations and puzzle sequences
notable_spikes: Expert difficulty mode removes visual note indicators, requiring play-by-ear expertise
Narrative climax involving pattern unraveling and protagonist destiny

notable_level_patterns.description: Spaces where player encounters objects demonstrating draft properties; examining blade being sharpened teaches 'Sharpening' draft; learning is voluntary but gated by presence of demonstrating object

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Puzzle gates locked by spell knowledge
notable_level_patterns.description: Environmental challenges requiring specific drafts; progression blocked until player observes and learns prerequisite spells
Difficulty settings (Standard showing note scale, Expert requiring play-by-ear) create replayability and skill-based progression independent of story
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All areas; selectable at game start
